name: Sol-Gel Synthesis
description: Sol-gel processing converts metal alkoxide precursors into an oxide network through hydrolysis and condensation: a colloidal sol crosslinks into a continuous gel, which is aged and then dried either supercritically to an aerogel or by evaporation to a xerogel, and finally calcined to the dense oxide.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Metal alkoxide precursor"]
    N2["Hydrolysis (M-OR -> M-OH)"]
    N3["Condensation (M-O-M)"]
    N4["Colloidal sol"]
    N5["Crosslink to gel network"]
    N6{"Gel point reached?"}
    N7["Age (more condensation)"]
    N8{"Drying route?"}
    N9["Supercritical -> aerogel"]
    N10["Evaporate -> xerogel"]
    N11["Calcine to oxide"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|age| N3
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 -->|supercritical| N9
    N8 -->|evaporative| N10
    N9 --> N11
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
